summary: Matched and cleaned up a first batch of effect-library decompilation work in src/melee/ef. The PR reports 100% matches for fn_8005DB70, efAsync_8006730C, efLib_8005BBB4, efLib_8005F454, and efLib_8005C1B4, and an improvement of efAsync_8006737C to 93.3%. Changes were concentrated in efasync.c, eflib.c, and eflib.h, mostly replacing scratch-style temporaries and commented-out experiments with cleaner control flow, exact casts, argument-width fixes, and header prototypes that better reflect the matching code.
what_changed: efAsync_8006730C was simplified by removing separate local copies of ef_DAT_file and effDataTable_name and directly using fields from the HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress result.
efAsync_8006730C changed the non-null test from bool-casts to a bitwise OR over u32-cast pointer fields before calling psInitDataBankLocate.

efAsync_8006737C was restructured around an int local copy of the s8 argument, early returns for out-of-range, negative, missing ef_DAT_file, or already-loaded unk8 cases, and localized temporaries around lbArchive_80017040 and psInitDataBankLoad.
efAsync_8006737C now assigns temp_r31->unk8 using pointer arithmetic, specifically (void*) ((u8*) spC + 8), rather than copying spC->unk8 directly.
efLib_8005BBB4 was rewritten from scratch-like duplicated variables and commented experiments into two straightforward HSD_GObj list loops over HSD_GObj_Entities->x2C and x30.
efLib_8005BBB4 keeps unused assignments from arg1 and arg8 to local variables, apparently for matching/register-shaping, but removes the unused values from the OSReport call.
efLib_8005BBB4 now returns early when efLib_804D64EC drops below 0x40 after efLib_8005B780, otherwise reports and asserts after both effect lists are exhausted.
efLib_8005C1B4 was tightened by combining GET_JOBJ(eff_1->gobj) into the null-check assignment and by using eff_1->gobj directly when unlinking with HSD_GObjPLink_80390228.
efLib_8005D174's first parameter type was changed from s8 to int in both eflib.c and eflib.h.
fn_8005DB70's header declaration was cleaned up into a single prototype with argument names: int arg0, int lo, int hi, HSD_JObj* jobj.
efLib_8005F454 had local declaration order adjusted, a stale comment about fighter special state removed, and its header prototype gained the arg_effect parameter name.
The PR removed several commented-out failed matching attempts and scratch notes while retaining an existing note around efLib_8005D174's inline-matching difficulty and decomp.me scratch reference.
smart_moves: Used early returns in efAsync_8006737C to flatten nested conditionals while preserving the guard order needed for matching.
Preserved argument values in unused locals in efLib_8005BBB4, which is a common decomp matching tactic when parameters affect register allocation even if the source-level logic does not use them.
Changed pointer truth tests to bitwise OR on integer-cast pointers in efAsync functions, matching the generated code pattern more closely than logical bool expressions.
Reduced temporary variables in efAsync_8006730C and efLib_8005C1B4, making the source cleaner without sacrificing the reported 100% matches.
Aligned header prototypes with matched call requirements, especially using int parameters for fn_8005DB70 and efLib_8005D174 instead of narrower s8/s32 variants.

Kept the function pointer callback-sensitive fn_8005DB70 signature as int/int/int/HSD_JObj*, reflecting the diff note that int argument types were required for compatibility with HSD_JObjSetDPtclCallback.

Replaced a field-copy assignment with explicit pointer arithmetic for temp_r31->unk8 in efAsync_8006737C, suggesting the original code likely treated the loaded struct layout/address directly rather than just copying the member value.
decomp_lessons: For effect async archive loaders, pointer presence checks may need integer bitwise OR expressions rather than idiomatic logical OR or bool-casts to match CodeWarrior output.
When a function accepts a narrow argument such as s8 but indexing/control flow matches poorly, assigning it into an int local can recover the expected sign-extension and register behavior.
Header prototypes are part of matching: changing s8 to int in efLib_8005D174 and using int parameters for fn_8005DB70 mattered enough to update eflib.h.
Do not over-preserve scratch temporaries once a match is found; this PR shows several functions becoming both cleaner and more matched after removing temp_ret-style comments and redundant locals.
When iterating GObj lists while potentially removing or mutating nodes, save next before calling the mutating helper, as efLib_8005BBB4 does before efLib_8005B780.
Callback-compatible function signatures can require exact C integer types even if earlier inferred prototypes used s32 or narrower aliases.
Struct layout details can affect matching: assigning a pointer to spC plus an offset may be necessary where a direct field assignment looks semantically similar but compiles differently.
naming_conventions: Effect-library functions continue using the efLib_8005XXXX address-based naming convention until semantic names are known.
Effect async functions continue using efAsync_80067XXX address-based names.
Anonymous or unresolved functions retain fn_8005XXXX names, as with fn_8005DB70.
Header prototypes may include useful argument names even for address-named functions, e.g. fn_8005DB70(int arg0, int lo, int hi, HSD_JObj* jobj) and efLib_8005F454(Effect* arg_effect).
Cleaner local names replaced compiler-scratch names where possible, such as result, gobj, next, jobj_1, jobj_2, DAT_filename, and effDataTable_name.
The diff preserves domain spelling used by existing structs and fields, including ef_DAT_file and effDataTable_name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use integer casts on pointers before bitwise OR to force non-short-circuit pointer-presence tests: (u32) ptr_a | (u32) ptr_b.
Introduce an int local copy of a narrow parameter to influence sign extension, indexing, and comparison codegen.
Flatten nested range and null checks into early returns when that better matches branch structure.
Save next = gobj->next before calling a function that may remove or mutate the current GObj.
Retain apparently unused locals initialized from parameters when needed for register allocation or ABI/codegen shape.
Use assignment inside a condition, e.g. if ((jobj_1 = GET_JOBJ(eff_1->gobj)) == NULL), when it helps generate the expected temporary lifetime.
Prefer direct field access through the original object over extra local aliases when the aliasing changes codegen.
Use explicit pointer arithmetic such as (void*) ((u8*) spC + 8) when the compiler must emit an address calculation rather than a field load.
Adjust function prototypes in headers to exact C types, not just same-size aliases, when callsite or callback matching depends on them.
Remove stale commented-out attempted code after matching to keep future decomp work from copying dead tactics.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body states the test plan was completed: builds successfully and clang-format passes.
The only human-visible summary evidence reports exact match percentages for the targeted functions and identifies this as part 1 of 3 for effect library functions.
searchable_terms: ef match effect library functions part 1
int arg0 int lo int hi HSD_JObj jobj
follow_up_queries: Find parts 2 and 3 of the effect library function matching series and compare which efLib functions were completed there.
Search for later semantic renames of efLib_8005BBB4, efLib_8005C1B4, efLib_8005F454, efAsync_8006730C, and efAsync_8006737C.
Check whether efAsync_8006737C eventually reached 100% and whether the pointer arithmetic assignment to unk8 remained.
Inspect callback callsites for fn_8005DB70 and HSD_JObjSetDPtclCallback to confirm the int parameter requirement.
Review later struct definitions around _struct_efAsync_803C025C_0xC, ef_DAT_file, effDataTable_name, and unk8 for naming or layout improvements.
